title: Explain the state-machine reason for checkout-then-mode ordering
prompt: Write `docs/ordering_state_machine.md` explaining in detail why `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.checkout/2` must precede `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.mode/2` when transitioning to shared mode. Must explain: (1) the sandbox is implemented as a GenServer that holds an 'owner' field, (2) `checkout/2` establishes the test process as the owner, (3) `mode({:shared, pid})` transitions the GenServer state to 'share connections from owner pid', (4) if you call mode before checkout, the GenServer has no owner state set and silently uses a stale or nil owner, (5) the race manifests as 'cannot find ownership process' in spawned children.
tier_rationale: Deep conceptual explanation — requires understanding the sandbox as a state-holding GenServer.
research_citation: "For shared mode the checkout actually has to happen first. The checkout requests go through a GenServer which will have its state set with the owner's connection when changing the mode to shared" — Elixir Forum, joey_the_snake
